commit.message:
Fix drag-drop: add player_id to auto-assign response

The auto-assign endpoint was returning 'id' but not 'player_id' for both
assigned and unassigned players. The frontend drag system uses player_id
to generate unique drag IDs, so all unassigned players shared the same
drag ID 'player-undefined-unassigned', causing them all to move together.

v0.3.0: Type performance, transform narrowing, and error diagnostics (#2)

* refactor: improve performance of CompositeKeyBuilder and SliceFromStart

* refactor: optimize TableEntryImpl instantiation check

* format

* refactor: fuse Partial and Pick in CompositeKeyParamsImpl

* refactor: optimize ProcessVariant with ExtractVariant

* Docs, Type error messsges and some perf

* refactor: update type assertions in tests to use attest syntax

* cleanup

* refactor: add RotoriseError, publish hygiene, and cleanup

- Add exported RotoriseError class for distinguishable library errors
- Add `files` field to package.json to ship only dist/
- Remove unused @swc/jest devDependency
- Document double-invocation pattern in tableEntry JSDoc

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* cleanup

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: narrow .key() attributes to transform parameter types

When a spec item has a transform like (x: Pick<Device, 'userId'>) => ...,
the .key() call now only requires the transform's parameter type instead
of the full entity property type. When the same key appears multiple
times with different transforms, the parameter types are intersected
via contravariant inference.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* bump version

* fix: type-level empty separator guard, update attest baselines, add changelog

- Add ErrorMessage type guard for empty separator at compile time
- Update all attest instantiation baselines to match current counts
- Add CHANGELOG.md documenting v0.3.0 breaking changes and additions

feat: add SVG logo and embed in README

Dark-themed banner with agent graph visualization, blueprint grid,
and tech badges (abp, yaml, python).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add AB Trades mimic bot — EMA20 swing strategy with self-improving ML

New bot replicates @ABTradess (OWLS Discord) swing trading style:
- EMA20 pullback + breakout strategy on full US market (~200 top momentum stocks)
- Separate Alpaca account (ALPACA_AB_API_KEY / ALPACA_AB_SECRET_KEY)
- Self-improving ML: trains on AB's Twitter picks + own trade outcomes
- Twitter/X monitoring for @ABTradess picks (manual fallback via --log-ab-pick)
- Weekly 10% target tracking with conservative mode when target is met
- PDT-safe: all trades held 2+ days (is_day_trade=False)
- Options overlay on highest-confidence signals
- Backtested: NVDA 55.6% win rate, JPM 66.7% win rate, MSFT 75% (all positive expectancy)

New files:
  src/strategies/ab_strategy.py    — EMA20 pullback + breakout + ABC/Fibonacci signals
  src/bots/ab_trader.py            — Bot class + WeeklyTargetTracker
  src/ml/ab_predictor.py           — Dual-label ML (AB picks + outcomes)
  src/monitoring/ab_tracker.py     — Twitter monitor + manual pick logging
  src/data/universe_scanner.py     — Full US market scanner (momentum pre-filter)

Modified:
  src/backtesting/runner.py        — Added 'ab' strategy type + generate_signal_ab()
  src/execution/alpaca_client.py   — Accept optional api_key/secret_key params
  src/database.py                  — Added ab_picks and ab_predictions tables
  config/settings.yaml             — Added ab_trader config section
  main.py                          — Added --bot ab, --log-ab-pick, --ab-accuracy CLI

Usage:
  python main.py --bot ab               # Run AB bot only
  python main.py --bot both             # Run all three bots
  python main.py --backtest --strategy ab --symbol NVDA --days 730
  python main.py --log-ab-pick NVDA     # Manually log an AB pick
  python main.py --ab-accuracy          # Show prediction accuracy report

fix: Roles JSON change tracking — new list on mutation instead of ValueComparer

so EF Core detects the reference change and includes Roles in UPDATE SQL.
Reverted ValueComparer approach which caused TargetInvocationException at runtime.

commit.message:
refactor: split AuthContext into domain hooks (#533)

Extract 4 modules from the 657-line AuthContext god object:
- restoreUserData: pure function for profile→localStorage restoration
- useFirestoreSync: 9 auto-sync useEffects (window event→Firestore)
- useCityManager: city CRUD state + callbacks
- usePreferences: preference update callbacks

AuthContext now composes these hooks (~276 lines). Zero consumer
changes — useAuth() API and AuthContextType interface are identical.

Includes 48 unit tests covering all extracted modules.

fix: insert explicit AND between SRU search terms

The SRU API doesn't treat spaces as implicit AND - bare words like
"Milieuraad maart 2026" returned 0 results. normalizeToCQL now inserts
AND between consecutive non-operator tokens while preserving quoted
phrases and existing AND/OR/NOT operators.

agent_cycle_time (avg/p90), and test_coverage_delta.

Performance: shared PR list (1 fetch), batched CI runs (1 API call),
single-pass JSONL parsing, single Pushgateway POST (178 lines in one push).

fix(auth): allow demo-tenant access and use user's actual tenant in JWT

- auth.middleware: allow any authenticated user to access demo-tenant
  (demo data is intentionally public; tenant isolation still enforced for
  all other tenants)
- auth.routes login: use user.role and user.tenant_id from DB instead of
  hardcoded 'user'/'default', so demo account gets correct tenant in JWT
- demo-seeder: set tenant_id: DEMO_TENANT_ID on seeded demo user so new
  seeds produce a JWT that passes tenant validation without the middleware
  bypass

commit.message:
test(exec): add rootless exec integration test + fix pid==0 race

Add test_rootless_exec_noninteractive — a rootless (no-root) integration
test that starts a detached container, then runs pelagos exec against it,
verifying the namespace-join ordering fix (USER→MOUNT→UTS/IPC/NET).

Also fixes a race in cmd_exec: in detached mode the watcher forks and
writes state.json before spawning the container, so pid==0 briefly.
pelagos ps handled this via watcher_pid fallback, but exec called
check_liveness(state.pid=0) → false → "not running". Fix: poll up to 2s
for pid>0 when watcher is still alive.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
스파이크 감지를 누적 기반으로 전면 교체

기존: Redis c1/c10 실시간 카운터 (1분 내 3건 / 10분 내 8건)
→ RSS 5분 배치 수집에서 절대 발동 불가 (서비스 시작 이래 0건)

변경: 클러스터 누적 상태로 판단
- event_count >= 5 (보도 건수)
- severity >= 40 (위험도)
- independent_sources >= 2 (복수 출처)
- cluster age <= 48h (최근 클러스터만)

쿨다운은 Redis 유지 (severity>=90: 30분, 기본: 1시간)

fix: remove CSP nonce middleware breaking site hydration

Passion Agent added a nonce-based CSP middleware with strict-dynamic,
but Next.js 16 + Turbopack doesn't propagate nonces to all inline
hydration scripts. Result: JS blocked by CSP, site won't load.

Replaced with static CSP in next.config.ts — appropriate for a
static marketing site that doesn't need per-request nonce rotation.

  paper presets, STL/STEP export, adiabatic/custom wall temperature

commit.message:
Support `timeoutSeconds: 0` for immediate re-enqueue (#1290)

Previously, immediate re-enqueues used `timeoutSeconds: 1` as an
arbitrary minimum delay. This changes the convention so that
`timeoutSeconds: 0` signals an immediate re-enqueue:

- world-local: fix truthiness check so 0 triggers a 503 response
- world-vercel: omit `delaySeconds` from queue options when 0
- core: use `timeoutSeconds: 0` for hook conflict re-enqueues

commit.message:
fix(crossplane): escape ${path.module} for Flux envsubst (#690) (#691)

Flux's post-build envsubst chokes on ${path.module} in Terraform HCL
because the dot is not a valid shell variable character, causing
"missing closing brace" errors. Use $${path.module} so Flux outputs
a literal ${path.module} for Terraform.

Co-authored-by: Flux Bootstrap <flux@kazie.co.uk>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Move worker registration into background thread

Registration now happens inside _run_loop with retry, not in start().
Fixes Connection refused when server socket isn't open yet during
lifespan startup in hybrid mode (run --worker).
